target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Update SDK runtime docs so the public plugin-state contract matches the new 3000-row cap and current-namespace eviction behavior.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.

## Verification

Latest local checks below were run on rebased head `ad603e40d4` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- Runtime plugin-state probe with the real SQLite store at the 3000-row Telegram shape
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`

Earlier before the final rebase, the focused plugin-state unit suite and Telegram message-cache suite also passed on the same PR diff: `23 passed` and `16 passed` respectively.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on rebased PR head `ad603e40d4`, existing system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API, and the real plugin-state SQLite store.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows in a temporary plugin-state SQLite DB, opened the real Telegram message-cache keyed store with `maxEntries: 3000`, then registered one new message-cache key `msg-new`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal output from the real runtime probe:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=ad603e40d4
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=610304
```

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows. The new Telegram message-cache row succeeds, exactly one older message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 topic-name-cache rows remain present, and total live rows stay within the 3000-row plugin cap.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.
